Weekly News — 2026-03-25
Private equity dealmaking surged to a six-year confidence high with $2.6 trillion in dry powder fueling megadeals across strategic sectors, while the Pentagon awarded $28.3 billion in contracts dominated by non-traditional defense primes like Anduril, SpaceX, and Palantir amid an escalating Iran campaign requiring a potential $200 billion supplemental. The Fed held rates steady at 3.5%-3.75% as oil prices topped $100/barrel from Middle East tensions, and progressive groups launched a $50 million midterm offensive targeting 35 Republican House seats.

Weekly News — 2026-03-25
The week was dominated by the intersection of the Iran conflict with global monetary and energy architecture, as the Fed held rates amid war-driven inflation uncertainty, Treasury Secretary Bessent moved to flood oil markets by lifting Iranian sanctions, and OPEC+ began unwinding production cuts. Russia signaled preparations for a potential 2026 escalation toward Kaliningrad while intensifying gray-zone operations against NATO, and Iran's IRGC consolidated control over national security policy with a hardline appointment to the Supreme National Security Council. The Murdoch family trust settlement locked in Fox Corp's editorial direction for a generation, while V-Dem's designation of the U.S. as a newly autocratizing country underscored deepening Western institutional strain.

Weekly News — 2026-03-25
The week was dominated by the intersection of the Iran conflict with global monetary and energy architecture, as the Fed held rates amid war-driven inflation uncertainty, Treasury Secretary Bessent moved to flood oil markets by lifting Iranian sanctions, and OPEC+ began unwinding production cuts. Russia signaled preparations for a potential 2026 escalation toward Kaliningrad while intensifying gray-zone operations against NATO, and Iran's IRGC consolidated control over national security policy with a hardline appointment to the Supreme National Security Council. The Murdoch family trust settlement locked in Fox Corp's editorial direction for a generation, while V-Dem's designation of the U.S. as a newly autocratizing country underscored deepening Western institutional strain.
